title: Storage systems, methods and containers
abstract: A storage system is described where goods are stored in containers (10) and the containers (10) are stored in stacks (12). Above the stacks (12) runs a grid network of tracks on which load handling devices (30) run. The load handling devices take containers (10) from the stacks (12) and deposit then at alternative locations in the stacks or deposit then at stations where goods may be picked out. The containers (10) comprise liners (50) formed from flame retardant or flame suppressant material. Containers (10) comprising liner means (50) are used to store ignitable items within the storage system.
claims: Claims 1 . A storage system comprising: a first set 22a of parallel rails 22 or tracks and a second set 22b of parallel rails 22 or tracks extending transversely to the first set 22a in a substantially horizontal plane to form a grid pattern comprising a plurality of grid spaces; a plurality of storage containers 10 arranged in stacks 12, located beneath the rails 22 and; at least one load handling device 30 disposed on the grid, arranged to move laterally above the stacks 12 on the rails 22, the load handling device 30 comprising a lifting device arranged to lift one or more containers 10 from a stack 12; wherein a number of the containers 10 comprise liner means 50 the liner means 50 acting so as to protect the storage system from items 28 contained within the container 10 that may pose a safety risk. 2. A storage system according to claim 1 in which the liner means 50 comprises at least one liner formed so as to substantially conform to the internal surface of the container 10, the liner acting so as to protect the container 10 from products contained therein. 3. A storage system according to claim 1 or 2 in which the liner means 50 comprises two co-operating liners 50 acting together so as to conform substantially to the inside surface of the container 10. 4. A storage system according to any preceding claim in which the liner means 50 comprises material capable of preventing the spread of fire in the event of a product 28 contained within the container combusting. 5. A storage system according to any preceding claim in which the liner means 50 comprises a metal liner. 6. A storage system according to claim 5 in which the metal liner comprises aluminium, an aluminium alloy, steel or any other suitable metal. 7. A storage system according to any one of claims 1 to 4 in which the liner means 50 comprises a flame retardant plastics or composite liner. 8. A storage system according to claim 7 in which the liner means 50 comprises PVC or glass reinforced plastics composite material. 9. A method of storing inflammable materials in a storage system, the storage system comprising a first set 22a of parallel rails 22 or tracks and a second set 22b of parallel rails 22 or tracks extending transversely to the first set 22a in a substantially horizontal plane to form a grid pattern comprising a plurality of grid spaces; a plurality of storage containers 10 arranged in stacks 12, located beneath the rails 22 and; at least one load handling device 30 disposed on the grid, arranged to move laterally above the stacks 12 on the rails 22, the load handling device 30 comprising a lifting device arranged to lift one or more containers 10 from a stack 12; comprising the steps of: lining at least one container 10 with flame retardant liner means 50; and loading inflammable items 28 in a container 10 comprising said liner 50 such that inflammable items stored within the container are not in direct contact therewith. 10. A method according to claim 9 in which the liner means 50 comprises at least one liner formed so as to substantially conform to the internal surface of the container 10, the liner acting so as to protect the container 10 from products contained therein. 1 1 . A method according to claim 9 or 10 in which the liner means 50 comprises two co-operating liners 50 acting together so as to conform substantially to the inside surface of the container 10. 12. A method according to any one of claims 9 to 1 1 in which the liner means 50 comprises material capable of preventing the spread of fire in the event of a product 28 contained within the container combusting. 13. A method according any one of claims 9 to 12 in which the liner means 50 comprises a metal liner. 14. A method according to claim 13 in which the metal liner comprises aluminium, an aluminium alloy, steel or any other suitable metal. 15. A method according to any one of claims 9 to 12 in which the liner means 50 comprises a flame retardant plastics or composite liner. 16. A method according to claim 15 in which the liner means 50 comprises PVC or glass reinforced plastics composite material. 17. A container 10 for use in a grid based storage system in which the container comprises liner means 50, the liner means 50 comprising flame retardant or flame suppressant material, in which the storage system forms part of a fulfilment system of an online retail organisation.
